Example Text: 
"Albert Einstein, the genius often associated with wild hair and mind-bending theories, famously won the Nobel Prize in Physics—though not for his groundbreaking work on relativity, as many assume. Instead, in 1968, he was honored for his discovery of the photoelectric effect, a phenomenon that laid the foundation for quantum mechanics."

**
IMPORTANT: Please make sure to only return in JSON format, with the "truths" key as a list of strings. No words or explanation is needed.

Text:
# Meridian Robotics — Operations & Safety Policy

## Pre-flight checks
Every inspection flight requires a two-person crew: a pilot and a safety observer. The
pilot completes a 12-point pre-flight checklist before takeoff. If wind speed at the
turbine hub exceeds the drone's rated tolerance, the flight is cancelled — no exceptions.

## No-fly conditions
Meridian drones do not fly in the following conditions: lightning within 10 kilometres,
visibility below 500 metres, or icing conditions on the turbine blades. The safety
observer has unilateral authority to abort any flight.

## Data handling
Inspection footage is uploaded to TurbineSight over an encrypted link within 24 hours of
each flight. Raw footage is retained for seven years to meet client audit requirements.
Personal data is never collected during inspections; the drones are pointed at turbines,
not people.

## Incident history
Meridian has recorded three minor incidents since 2019, all involving hard landings in
gusty conditions. No injuries have ever occurred. The full incident log is confidential
and is not shared outside the safety team.

## Kestrel-2
The Kestrel-2, released in 2023, is the flagship model. It extends flight time to 55
minutes and raises wind tolerance to 22 metres per second. The Kestrel-2 adds a thermal
imaging camera alongside the visible-light camera, allowing it to detect overheating
components inside turbine nacelles.

## TurbineSight
TurbineSight is Meridian's cloud software for reviewing inspection footage. It runs a
defect-detection model that flags blade cracks, lightning strike damage, and corrosion.
TurbineSight is sold on an annual subscription; pricing is negotiated per customer and
is not published.
